title: poa alberti subsp. lahulensis
intro: Poa alberti subsp. lahulensis is a subspecies of grass native to the Himalayan region, specifically found in the Lahul valley of India. It is believed to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory problems, and is also known to have antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of Poa alberti subsp. lahulensis include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
